---
title: '2014/04/04 Journal Fri'
date: '2014-04-04T12:59:00.0030000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: '(My Journal) Fed By Faith'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '1025970478325079081'
blogger_filename: '/2014/04/20140404-journal-fri.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/(My Journal) Fed By Faith/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Journal'
---

<br />
<h4>
VOD:</h4>
COME:<br />
<br />
<h3 class="hd" style="-webkit-background-clip: padding; -webkit-box-shadow: rgb(255, 255, 255) 0px 1px 0px 0px inset; background-clip: padding-box; background-color: #dddcda; background-image: linear-gradient(rgb(238, 237, 235), rgb(221, 220, 218)); background-size: 100%; border-bottom-color: rgb(203, 201, 207); border-bottom-style: solid; border-top-left-radius: 4px; border-top-right-radius: 4px; border-width: 0px 0px 1px; box-shadow: rgb(255, 255, 255) 0px 1px 0px 0px inset; color: #333333; font-family: 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, 'Liberation Sans', FreeSans, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; line-height: 24px; margin: 0px; padding: 5px 15px; position: relative;">
<a href="https://www.bible.com/users/darrellwolfe/reading-plans/28-joseph-prince?initial=true&amp;user_id=darrellwolfe" target="_blank">Devotional</a></h3>
<div class="bd" style="-webkit-background-clip: padding; background-color: #eeedeb; background-image: url(https://web-production.s3.amazonaws.com/assets/shadow-4d512a7dd93d0b3310411703391b99c1.png); background-repeat: repeat no-repeat; border: 0px; color: #333333; font-family: 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, 'Liberation Sans', FreeSans, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: 21.600000381469727px; margin: 0px; padding: 20px;">
<div style="-webkit-background-clip: padding; background-clip: padding-box; border: 0px; font-size: 12px; margin-bottom: 20px; padding: 0px;">
Day 143</div>
<div style="-webkit-background-clip: padding; background-clip: padding-box; border: 0px; font-size: 12px; margin-bottom: 20px; padding: 0px;">
God Will Surely Show You Kindness</div>
<div style="-webkit-background-clip: padding; background-clip: padding-box; border: 0px; font-size: 12px; margin-bottom: 20px; padding: 0px;">
2 Samuel 9:7</div>
<div style="-webkit-background-clip: padding; background-clip: padding-box; border: 0px; font-size: 12px; margin-bottom: 20px; padding: 0px;">
7So David said to him, “Do not fear, for I will surely show you kindness for Jonathan your father’s sake, and will restore to you all the land of Saul your grandfather; and you shall eat bread at my table continually.”</div>
<div style="-webkit-background-clip: padding; background-clip: padding-box; border: 0px; font-size: 12px; margin-bottom: 20px; padding: 0px;">
When the people in the palace heard that King Saul and his son Jonathan had died in battle, they panicked. Fearful that David was coming to seize the throne, and kill all the sons and grandsons of Saul, they ran for their lives. A nurse took Mephibosheth, Jonathan’s son, and ran. But as she fled, the five-year-old boy fell and became lame in both feet. (2 Samuel 4:4)</div>
<div style="-webkit-background-clip: padding; background-clip: padding-box; border: 0px; font-size: 12px; margin-bottom: 20px; padding: 0px;">
The poor boy would not have been crippled if they had known that David actually loved Jonathan and Saul, and would have looked after Jonathan’s son because he had made a covenant with Jonathan. (1 Samuel 18:3)</div>
<div style="-webkit-background-clip: padding; background-clip: padding-box; border: 0px; font-size: 12px; margin-bottom: 20px; padding: 0px;">
In this story, King Saul represents the human race. He is like Adam who sinned against God and forfeited all that God gave to man. He had the position, but not the power anymore because of sin. Jonathan, who came after Saul, represents Jesus, who came as a human. Jonathan was not like Saul — he was good, just as Jesus is not like Adam — Jesus is the perfect Man. David represents God. So Jonathan’s covenant with David speaks of Jesus’ covenant with God. Mephibosheth represents believers today who are “lame” in some way — sick, depressed, fearful, poor and so on.</div>
<div style="-webkit-background-clip: padding; background-clip: padding-box; border: 0px; font-size: 12px; margin-bottom: 20px; padding: 0px;">
Now, Mephibosheth became lame because of bad news that was based on a lie. Many believers today are suffering needlessly because they believe the wrong things about God. They think, “My sin has found me out! God is coming after me!”</div>
<div style="-webkit-background-clip: padding; background-clip: padding-box; border: 0px; font-size: 12px; margin-bottom: 20px; padding: 0px;">
Well, I have good news for all Mephibosheths: David is not after your life! When David looked for Mephibosheth, it was to show him kindness, to take care of him and restore to him the land that had belonged to Saul his grandfather.</div>
<div style="-webkit-background-clip: padding; background-clip: padding-box; border: 0px; font-size: 12px; margin-bottom: 20px; padding: 0px;">
Likewise, God is not out to get you. He knows all about your sins and still loves you. That is why He sent Jesus as the payment for your sins. And because of Jesus’ covenant with Him, He says to you, “Don’t be afraid, I will surely show you kindness. I will restore all to you and you shall always eat at My table!”</div>
<div style="-webkit-background-clip: padding; background-clip: padding-box; border: 0px; font-size: 12px; margin-bottom: 20px; padding: 0px;">
Thought For The Day</div>
<div style="-webkit-background-clip: padding; background-clip: padding-box; border: 0px; font-size: 12px; margin-bottom: 20px; padding: 0px;">
God is not out to get you, but to show you kindness, and to give you provision and restoration.</div>
</div>
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
HERE:<br />
<br />
WOW! That has got to be one of the coolest revelations I've ever heard! I am Mephibosheth!<br />
<br />
I'm that lame one who is hiding in fear from God's plan and He is seeking me out to show me great kindness because of a covenant struck with another. Because of Jesus I inherit this blessing!<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
DO:<br />
<br />
Today I will ask God for His plan in my life, and really make sure the plan I follow is His, and not a creation of my own.<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<h4>
Thankful:</h4>
<br />
I'm thankful for Flavia's insights. She sees well.<br />
<br />
I'm thankful for God's help yesterday with the fence!<br />
<br />
I'm thankful for my new garden and Flavia's ideas for that too.<br />
<br />
<h4>
Feeling:</h4>
I'm feeling nervous that TD Ameritrade hasn't called back yet. If they don't hire me I'll assume God has another plan. But that job seemed so perfect. Location, Culture, Benefits, New Skill Set Learned, Close Enough to my current skill set to be a match for them. I'm actually kind of bummed that the job offer didn't come right away.<br />
<br />
And yet, I haven't got a decline letter either. Maybe this is part of that faith journey. I'm giving up "Hope" - "Expectation" before any natural decline letter has come. Further more, if I KNEW it was God the decline letter wouldn't even be the end of it. If I didn't KNOW it was God why was I expecting it in the first place? I can only rightly expect what He has promised.<br />
<br />
Unfounded Expectations Lead To Unexpected Disappointment and Discouragement.<br />
<br />
<br />
<h4>
What's Up?</h4>
<br />
So, as I said I'm still waiting for word from TDA.<br />
<br />
I replaced yet another fence post last two days. Did one last year too. I cleaned out the garden in front. It's going to be fabulous! We're going to plant another rose bush. Then three Artichoke Plants. Next row will be asparagus. Final row will be succulents of various kinds. I think we need at least one aloe.<br />
<br />
I've started a study of end times prophecy. I'm not starting just because I want to find a niche site. But I do believe that may be what it becomes. This is something I know, have a skill for, and a passion for. End Times Prophecy. Another thing I have a passion for, but not specific skill/education in, is the science of Creation vs Evolution. rather than start a site for that, as I don't have enough valuable to add to make that a site, I may weave those tid bits into the larger discussion of End Times.<br />
<br />
I don't have much passion for the life in fort worth site. But I"m going to give that one year before I call it a side thing.<br />
<br />
<br />
I'm still finding my voice at DW.Com... but I can do whatever I want there. It's MY site! So I'm thinking of playing around some with the voice and topics. Eventually that experimentation will help me craft a new give away book, a sort of manifesto.<br />
<br />
I need a clear message, core to who I am, passionate, within my realm of expertise. I'm going to find that this year.<br />
<br />
<br />
<h4>
PRAYER:</h4>
<br />
Lord. Help me find my Sweet Spot. In the mean time, please give me a better job environment where I can grow but not be miserable every day. Make more money to support this family. Thank you.<br />
<br />
I guess those are me asking, which is ok, it's what I've been doing. It's occurring to me as I write this though, there might be a deeper, more meaningful ask. But this ask is more dangerous because it could lead anywhere, and more fear-inducing because it could mean hearing something I don't want to hear and doing something I don't want to do... Yet... that's absurd! I just read I'm Mephibosheth! You are looking for me to bless me! Psalms 23 reminds me I'll have a table in the presence of my enemies, even if you DO lead me through a dark valley! Psalms 103 reminds me of all your benefits! Deut 28 are the blessings I live by!<br />
<br />
<br />
Lord... here it is..<br />
<br />
"Do what YOU WILL with my life! What is YOUR PLAN for me, and how can I participate in it today?"<br />
<br />
That's not really it.<br />
<br />
<b>"Lord, I submit to YOUR PLAN, prescribed from before time for me. It is the best plan for me. What is your plan? How can I participate? What would you have me do today?"&nbsp;</b><br />
<br />
That's it.<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
Darrell G. Wolfe<br />
<br />
<h4>
</h4>
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
